description_long: This house features a rustic, medieval-inspired design with a prominent gabled roof covered in weathered shingles, some of which are overgrown with moss. The exterior walls are a mix of rough stonework on the lower half and whitewashed timber framing above, giving it a classic half-timbered appearance. Wooden shutters cover most of the windows, and a small fenced area is attached to one side, suggesting a yard or garden. The roofline is complex, with intersecting gables and a central raised section, adding architectural interest. A small stone chimney is visible on one side, and the entryway is sheltered by an overhanging roof supported by wooden posts. The overall look is that of a traditional European countryside dwelling, likely from the Tudor or medieval period.
description: A rustic, half-timbered house with a mossy, gabled roof, stone lower walls, and wooden shutters, evoking a medieval European style.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entryway with an overhanging gable, wooden posts, and a small fenced yard to the right.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the intersecting gabled roofs, multiple shuttered windows, and the fenced area extending from the house.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the central raised roof section, a small window, and the stone chimney on the right.
description_view_3: Angled view emphasizing the depth of the gabled roof and the stone foundation, with a clear look at the side window and yard.
attribute_entryway_design: Covered entry with overhanging gable, wooden posts, and a small adjacent fenced yard.
